steps.title: Identify the sample-size gap
steps.content: The passage requires 176 individuals for a representative result but notes that both Lockard and Goodall used only 8 individuals each. This disparity is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Apply the sample-size constraint
steps.content:
Choice A states that neither study provides sufficient evidence—the only conclusion consistent with both falling far short of the 176-individual minimum.

Choices B and D wrongly claim at least one study is reliable, and C invokes an unsupported directional assumption.

Since both studies fall equally short of the 176-individual standard, neither can provide the evidence needed for a meaningful comparison.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
